user_query:
I need a quick code-search driven audit of the small Python project under input/project/. Read only from input/project/ and do not modify any input files. Produce the following four outputs exactly:

1) output/reports/todo_report.json
- JSON array of objects with keys: path (relative to input/project), line (1-based), text (the TODO content only)
- Find every occurrence of the token "TODO" across all files (code and docs). For each, extract the text after "TODO" or "TODO:" (strip leading comment markers like # or //, remove the word TODO and any leading punctuation/whitespace). Keep original punctuation that comes after the extracted content.

2) output/reports/routes.csv
- Extract Flask route definitions from *.py files by locating lines that match @app.route("...") and the function defined immediately below
- CSV with header: route_path,function_name
- One row per route

3) output/reports/import_index.txt
- List all unique file paths (relative to input/project) that contain the word "import" (this includes lines like "from x import y" and "import x")
- One path per line
- Sort ascending (lexicographic)

4) output/reports/summary.md
- Exactly three lines, with counts consistent with the other outputs:
Total TODOs: N
Files with imports: M
Discovered routes: K

Use relative paths in outputs (e.g., app/main.py). Write to the exact output/ paths above.
